annotations.label: Euclidean group E(n)

anchor.snippet: a Euclidean group is the group of (Euclidean) isometries of a Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib has the group of affine isometric self-equivalences `AffineIsometryEquiv 𝕜 P P` (via `AffineIsometryEquiv.instGroup`) which instantiated at `ℝ` and `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)` gives E(n), but no decl is named the Euclidean group.

annotations.label: Contents of E(n)

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean group E( n ) comprises all translations

annotations.note: Translation-by-`v` is in Mathlib as `AffineIsometryEquiv.constVAdd`, but no statement asserts that translations, rotations and reflections together generate the Euclidean group.

annotations.label: Special Euclidean group SE(n)

anchor.snippet: The direct Euclidean isometries form a subgroup, the special Euclidean group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.specialOrthogonalGroup` for SO(n) but no special Euclidean group SE(n) of orientation-preserving affine isometries.

annotations.label: Degrees of freedom of E(n)

anchor.snippet: The number of degrees of freedom for E( n ) is n ( n + 1)/2

annotations.note: No formula for the dimension of the Euclidean isometry group is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direct isometries subgroup

anchor.snippet: The direct isometries (i.e., isometries preserving the handedness of chiral subsets) comprise a subgroup of E( n )

annotations.note: Orientation-preserving affine isometries are not isolated as a named subgroup in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Indirect isometries form a coset; E+(n) has index 2

anchor.snippet: the indirect isometries are a coset of E + ( n )

annotations.note: No statement that the direct-isometry subgroup has index 2 inside the Euclidean group is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convergence in E(n)

anchor.snippet: a sequence f i of isometries of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no topology or uniform structure on `IsometryEquiv` / `AffineIsometryEquiv` capturing pointwise convergence of isometries.

annotations.label: Continuous trajectory in E(n)

anchor.snippet: Such a function is called a "continuous trajectory" in E( n )

annotations.note: `Path` formalizes continuous maps `[0,1] → X` in general, but no topology on the Euclidean group is set up so `Path` cannot be instantiated as a continuous trajectory in E(n).

annotations.label: SE(n) is connected

anchor.snippet: the special Euclidean group SE( n ) = E + ( n ) is connected in this topology

annotations.note: Mathlib does not establish connectedness of any special Euclidean or special orthogonal group.

annotations.label: E(n) is not connected

anchor.snippet: the group E( n ) as a whole is not connected

annotations.note: No statement about disconnectedness of the Euclidean group appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous trajectories model rigid body motion

anchor.snippet: The continuous trajectories in E(3) play an important role in classical mechanics

annotations.note: Mathlib mentions rigid motions only in the dual-quaternion module docstring and does not formalize rigid-body motion as a path in E(3).

annotations.label: Euclidean groups are Lie groups

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean groups are not only topological groups , they are Lie groups

annotations.note: No `LieGroup` instance on the Euclidean isometry group (or the orthogonal group) is provided in Mathlib.

annotations.label: E(n) as subgroup of affine group / semidirect product

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean group E( n ) is a subgroup of the affine group for n dimensions

annotations.note: `AffineIsometryEquiv` is by definition built on top of `AffineEquiv`, encoding the inclusion of Euclidean isometries into affine maps, but the semidirect-product description is not stated.

annotations.label: Unique translation-then-orthogonal decomposition

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: Any element of E( n ) is a translation followed by an orthogonal transformation

annotations.note: No unique decomposition of an `AffineIsometryEquiv` into a translation composed with a linear orthogonal map is proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: T(n) is normal in E(n)

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: T( n ) is a normal subgroup of E( n )

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Euclidean-group translation subgroup, let alone a normality statement for it.

annotations.label: E(n) is semidirect product O(n) ⋉ T(n)

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: E( n ) is the semidirect product of O( n ) extended by T( n )

annotations.note: `SemidirectProduct` and `Matrix.orthogonalGroup` exist, but no isomorphism realizing E(n) as O(n) ⋉ T(n) is proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: E+(n) is index-two subgroup of direct isometries

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: E( n ) has a subgroup E + ( n ), also of index two, consisting of direct isometries

annotations.note: No index-2 subgroup of direct Euclidean isometries is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite subgroups have a fixed point

anchor.snippet: They always have a fixed point

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem that every finite subgroup of the Euclidean isometry group fixes a point (e.g. via averaging).

annotations.label: Discrete subgroups of E(n)

anchor.snippet: for every point the set of images under the isometries is topologically discrete

annotations.note: No notion of a discrete subgroup of the Euclidean group is set up in Mathlib (no topology on the group is even defined).

annotations.label: Non-closed-orbit subgroups

anchor.snippet: there are points for which the set of images under the isometries is not closed

annotations.note: No example of a subgroup of E(n) with non-closed orbits appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chasles' theorem

anchor.section: Overview of isometries in up to three dimensions
anchor.snippet: Chasles' theorem asserts that any element of E + (3) is a screw displacement

annotations.note: Grep for `Chasles` and `screw` in `Mathlib/` returns nothing; this classification of orientation-preserving rigid motions of 3-space is absent.

annotations.label: Commuting isometry pairs

anchor.snippet: For some isometry pairs composition does not depend on order

annotations.note: No classification of commuting pairs of Euclidean isometries is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Translation conjugacy class

anchor.snippet: The translations by a given distance in any direction form a conjugacy class

annotations.note: No statement that translations of given length form a single conjugacy class in the Euclidean group is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflections in 1D form one class

anchor.snippet: In 1D, all reflections are in the same class

annotations.note: Mathlib does not classify conjugacy classes of E(1).

annotations.label: 2D conjugacy classes

anchor.snippet: In 2D, rotations by the same angle in either direction are in the same class

annotations.note: Mathlib does not classify conjugacy classes of E(2).

annotations.label: 3D conjugacy classes

anchor.snippet: Inversions with respect to all points are in the same class

annotations.note: Mathlib does not classify conjugacy classes of E(3).
